artPrompt: The chilling instant of a hit: a goblin enforcer's silhouette looming in a rain-slick alley over a fallen rival's hat rolling on wet cobbles, a single wilting flower laid beside it, two favor tokens dropped in the puddle. A lone amber streetlamp throws hard light across the slick stones and the enforcer's grin, the rest devoured by ink-black noir. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Whack-a-Rival
text:
Destroy target creature. Its controller may prevent this by sacrificing two Treasures or doing you two Favors (you create two Favor tokens). They never have the cash on them. They never do.
This is not personal. It is, however, extremely efficient.
flavorText: The Don sent flowers to the funeral. The card read: "Nothing personal. — The Don."
